response.justification: Candidate is a senior/staff engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level junior Java role — a severe seniority mismatch that creates high flight risk and compensation misalignment. Java is listed only as a peripheral skill (robotic arms work years ago); the core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not Java. German proficiency is only basic, falling short of the strong German requirement for client-facing insurance consulting work. While technically very capable, the role profile does not fit.
strengths: Deep polyglot software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Strong agile, architecture, and cloud/Kubernetes exposure
Holds a Computer Science degree and active open-source contributor
concerns: Massive overqualification for an entry-level junior position
Java is a minor, dated part of the toolkit — primary languages are TS/Node and DLang
German listed as only Basic, below the strong German requirement

key_factors.factor: Seniority mismatch (15+ years vs. junior role)
Limited Java experience for a Java-focused role
German language only at basic level
